hook_type: Numbers + 'doing things differently' curiosity gap (forward-looking vision voice)
tactic: Proof cadence -> forward-looking modernisation reveal -> name Birchal Next -> waitlist
motivation: Vision + curiosity (what is changing, and why now)
primary_text:
After 324 raises and $234M+, we are doing things differently.

We built the platform Australians used to back Naked Life and Our Cow. From mid 2026 we are modernising it: better analytics, smoother onboarding, faster from prep to settlement. We call it Birchal Next. To fund it, we are opening our own Crowd-Sourced Funding raise.

The waitlist is free. You read the offer document before you decide.

Leave your email and we will tell you the moment the offer goes live.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: What comes next for the platform
description: Birchal Next is coming. Save your spot to hear when.
_source_label: DRC V5 -- W2 Birchal Next / Vision (first-timers)
_iterates: BIRCHAL-V4-A1 structure; new BIRCHAL-NEXT positioning angle the client introduced 2026-05-17.
_v5_rationale: Uses the BIRCHAL-NEXT fact in strictly forward-looking voice: 'From mid 2026 we are modernising it', 'we call it Birchal Next', 'the next chapter'. NEVER present-tense capability ('Birchal now has supreme analytics'). 'better analytics, smoother onboarding, faster from prep to settlement' is the client's stated plan rephrased into non-banned, future-framed language (avoids the banned AI-tells 'supreme'/'next-generation' from the verbatim positioning; those are exempt only inside the attributed verbatim, not in ad prose).

'doing things differently' is the client's own verbatim friend-pitch opener; kept because it is her positioning language and it opens a curiosity gap (what is different?). Hook Halbert 5/5 in --primary-text mode.

No 'backed by X and Y' claim included: per BIRCHAL-NEXT note, that placeholder must be replaced with sourced endorsement language only. W2 omits backer claims entirely (the investor-validation angle is carried by W4). Vision angle stays clean of any cap-table implication.

V5b: stronger email-capture CTA (FarmThru-proven pattern). Closing CTA changed from the weaker 'save your spot' framing to an explicit email-capture micro-action ('Leave your email...'), per clients/farm-thru/learnings-meta-ad.md verbatim ('Outcome-stated CTA: leave your email, we will tell you the moment it goes live'). Notification-framed, no transaction verb, AU English, no contractions, CSF-compliant. Risk-reversal sentence, hook, headline, proof cadence, fact citations, and the verbatim CSF warning all unchanged. Button cta field 'Save my spot' (approved WAITLIST-CTA verbatim) unchanged.
_drc_audience: B2C considered, solution-aware. First-time CSF investors who respond to a forward-looking platform story.
_compliance_findings_resolved: PREOFFER-VERBS: 'save your spot', 'we will tell you the moment'. No transaction verbs.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'we will tell you the moment the offer goes live'. No 'we will send'.
VERBATIM-CSF-WARNING: byte-exact final line, appears exactly once.
BRAND-EXCLUDED: only verified-good raises (Naked Life RAISE-001, Our Cow RAISE-002).

BIRCHAL-NEXT used as VISION only: all references forward-looking ('from mid 2026 we are modernising', 'the next chapter'), never proven-fact or present-state. No 'backed by X and Y' / no unsourced backer claim. Banned AI-tells from the verbatim positioning ('supreme', 'next-generation') not used in ad prose.
Subject discipline: 'Birchal' appears once, in 'Birchal Next' (product/positioning name). First-person we/our elsewhere. No platform-vs-company swap.
Number traceability: 324=PLAT-001, $234M+=PLAT-002, brands=RAISE-001/002. 'mid 2026' is from BIRCHAL-NEXT verbatim (client-stated timeframe), labelled forward-looking.
